methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.0B - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
Vannevar Labs is a Series B defense and security technology company founded by Brett Granberg and Nini Moorhead, currently valued at $1.5B post-money on $90M raised. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 companies: 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies operating in the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods apply to this valuation. Stage-progression modeling applies a typical 1.9x multiplier to the $1.5B Series B post-money valuation, implying $2.9B at the next financing stage. Peer-median analysis across 5 same-sector, same-stage private comparables—Dream Security ($1.5B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Castelion ($1.0B), Defense Unicorns ($1.0B), and Heven AeroTech ($1.0B)—yields a median of $1.0B with a range of $1.0B to $1.5B. The 5-peer median reflects lower valuations within the peer set relative to Vannevar's current valuation.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $850M (low) to $3.3B (high), with a median of $1.9B. The current $1.5B valuation sits below the median estimate, reflecting the peer-median method's lower signal. Key uncertainties include differentiation relative to the identified peer set and revenue or customer concentration relative to comparables. Stage-progression and peer-median methods diverge significantly, suggesting heightened sensitivity to company-specific execution factors.
